key_factors: Morocco is ranked 7th in the official FIFA rankings, while Canada is ranked 30th.
Canada reached the Round of 16 for the first time in their history after defeating South Africa 1-0, while Morocco advanced by defeating the Netherlands on penalties after a 1-1 draw.
Canada midfielder Ismaël Koné remains ruled out of the tournament with a broken leg, creating a confirmed key starter absence asymmetry in favor of Morocco.

Canada traveled approximately 1,380 miles from Los Angeles to Houston, while Morocco traveled approximately 400 miles from Monterrey. This 980-mile travel difference exceeds the 800-mile threshold and warrants a 2-point adjustment in favor of Morocco.
Hot and humid conditions (high of 97°F and heat index up to 109°F) are forecast in Houston, but the match is played inside the closed, air-conditioned Houston Stadium (no weather asymmetry).
The referee is English official Michael Oliver, who has issued an average of 3.5 yellow cards and 0.0 red cards per match in his 2 refereed games this tournament.
unverified_or_unknown: The physical fitness of Alphonso Davies for Canada is unconfirmed, as he recently returned from a hamstring injury and his starting status is under evaluation.
The injury status of Morocco's Nayef Aguerd and Abde Ezzalzouli is unconfirmed, as they are managing knocks and undergo late fitness tests.
The exact starting lineups and tactical approaches are not officially confirmed at prediction time.
